summary: Epic Dark Star marathon with soaring Morning Dew and Bird Song
blurb: The June 10 1973 RFK show delivers a jaw‑dropping Dark Star/He's Gone/Wharf Rat/Truckin' trek, a soaring Morning Dew opener and a glittering Bird Song jam. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Phil lays down a monster bass groove, and Keith adds lush texture throughout. It’s widely regarded as one of the most powerful three‑set outings of the era.
review:
June 10, 1973 at RFK Stadium captured the Grateful Dead at a creative peak. The night opened with an electrifying “Morning Dew” that set a mystical tone, then rolled through classics like “Box of Rain,” “Bird Song,” and a blistering “Playing In The Band.” Set 2 hit its apex with a 26‑minute “Dark Star” that flowed straight into “He's Gone,” “Wharf Rat,” and “Truckin’, ” delivering the kind of marathon jam that still makes fans gasp. The third set, bolstered by an Allman Brothers guest appearance, kept the energy high with “Sugar Magnolia” and a fiery “Not Fade Away.” Across all sources the performance is described as “legendary,” “magical,” and “on fire,” while the soundboard transfers (especially Miller’s fix) are praised for crystal‑clear fidelity that lets every nuance shine.

The band was locked in: Jerry’s Chuck‑Berry‑style tone roared through Dark Star and Eyes Of The World, Phil’s bass was a “monster” foundation, Keith’s keyboards wove harmonic depth, and Bill’s jazz‑inflected drumming drove the jams forward. Even with a few weaker moments (the brief “Race Is On” and some uneven first‑set spots), the show’s highs far outweigh any dips, making this a must‑listen for anyone chasing that 1973‑summer Dead magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Out of 11 recordings, 7 are strongly positive, praising the Dark Star marathon, Morning Dew opener and overall band lock‑in; only one mixed and one negative note have minimal impact. The consensus is overwhelmingly enthusiastic, justifying a +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Miller’s flawless soundboard transfer captures the show with crisp, clean fidelity; no dropouts and superb instrument separation make it the most listenable version.

key_highlights: Epic Dark Star > He's Gone > Wharf Rat > Truckin' marathon (26 min)
Soaring Morning Dew opener that sets a mystical tone
Magical Bird Song jam and tight Playing In The Band segue
Allman Brothers guest spot adding bluesy flavor to the third set

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly praised as ‘on fire,’ delivering soaring leads on Dark Star, Eyes Of The World and the marathon Dark Star/He's Gone/Wharf Rat/Truckin' sequence; his tone is described as Chuck‑Berry‑style yet expansive.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass provides a monster foundation throughout, highlighted on Dark Star, Morning Dew and the extended jams; reviewers note his solid presence and “tonal edge” that locks the band together.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s keyboards add lush harmonic support and a standout post‑verse solo on Bird Song, enhancing the overall texture without any major criticisms.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is lauded as jazz‑inflected and driving, especially during the second‑set jams; his solos add excitement and keep the rhythm tight.
